title: WireNet Manager v0.2 Routing

provenance.capture_rule: The contract describes the distributed plugin and generated canonical Manager runtime.

scope.workspace_model: An installed plugin owns behavior; a content-only local Manager owns personal memory; implementation work remains in external workspaces connected through device-local bindings.
scope.project_location: Portable Project Packs live in Manager/projects/<slug>; code, media, data, and operational files remain at bound external paths.
scope.okf: The Manager is one canonical OKF bundle with a transparent runtime overlay. index.md and log.md are reserved support documents; AGENTS.md is runtime; every other in-scope Markdown file requires a non-empty type. Project Packs are the intended portable synchronization units.

entities.description: Keeps device-local outputs and platform noise out of portable Manager history.
Typed Manager Overview and human entry point for this instance, its shelves, and local/portable boundary.
Manager-local read order, durable-state destinations, update threshold, and safety rules under the runtime schema; deliberately outside the OKF projection.
WireNet-required bundle catalog and OKF version declaration. OKF itself permits a bundle without it.
Ordered immediate stack, next commitments, waiting items, and planning-relevant later work.
Confirmed working style, responsibilities, operating boundaries, and human-readable workspace context.
Canonical shelf for portable Project Packs; navigation lives in projects/index.md and runtime rules in projects/AGENTS.md.
WireNet-required canonical progressive-disclosure catalog for active Project Packs. OKF itself permits it to be absent.
Defines the open packet core, shared project identity, additive indexes, optional extensions, portability, and log thresholds.
Current operational handoff: purpose, status, next move, owners, decisions, blockers, and canonical sources.
Project read order, recurring source routes, safety gates, and per-file update thresholds.
Stable outcome contract, baseline, completion criteria, constraints, non-goals, and approval gates.
Completed outcomes and verification evidence without duplicating active status.
Detailed UltraGoal attempts, evidence, failures, and next experiment while an explicitly activated durable goal is active.
Sparse, newest-first, meaningful state transitions. It is not a command log or a duplicate status document.
Shelf for typed, evidence-backed canonical collaborator concepts.
Canonical evidence-backed work relationship context: role, responsibilities, confirmed preferences, handoffs, and links to relevant projects.
Durable scratch notes without a better canonical home; stable project state is promoted into its packet.
consumed_by: Manager when no better canonical destination exists

entities.description: Curated Knowledge Shelf for retained evidence and source context, read-only by default and link-first; excludes private, large, or operational source trees.
Optional structured documents without a stronger project, person, or source home; no fixed taxonomy is imposed.
consumed_by: Manager when structured durable content needs a home

entities.description: Collection for bounded, decision-oriented Experiment Packs that remain lighter than durable projects.
Lifecycle-aware catalog of active, concluded, promoted, and archived Experiment Packs.
Bounded question, decision criterion, current observation, next move, and lifecycle state.
Experiment read order, bounded update rules, safety, and promotion guidance.
Durable observation, supporting evidence, and decision when the experiment earns a separate result.
Ignored device-local working memory for generated intermediates; never canonical or synchronized by default.
Inactive durable context preserved for history rather than deleted.
consumed_by: Manager when historical context is needed

entities.description: Manager behavior, including explicit-only UltraGoal, is versioned in an installable plugin and not copied into personal Manager content.

Current globally discoverable user-specific writing behavior generated from approved authored-message evidence or migrated from a grounded repo-local profile. It remains outside Manager knowledge and contains no raw source messages.
produced_by: write-like-me-bootstrap generation, migration, or refresh after explicit approval

entities.description: The canonical content-only Manager seed lives with the plugin so bootstrap and repair are versioned without copying procedural skills into personal content.
Maps stable Project and Experiment Pack IDs to machine-local paths and records ignored routes. It contains no instructions or portable prose.
Manager ID, schema, plugin version, Project Pack profile, and OKF profile.
Code, media, data, and operational work remain outside Manager and are connected by project ID and local path.
Read-only branded Google-derived graph-and-detail view of typed OKF concepts and real Markdown-link edges; reserved index.md, log.md, and runtime AGENTS.md do not enter the generated payload.

routes.trigger: The Manager skill is invoked or a task opens inside Manager.
routes.description: Resolve Manager, read the ordered current stack and catalog, then disclose only the relevant packet and sources.

routes.trigger: A meaningful durable project transition would otherwise make a future task resume incorrectly.
reads: optional concepts when present and relevant
writes: optional typed concept when it earns a distinct purpose
optional linked log.md entry when sparse chronology adds value
routes.approval: preview and explicit approval unless the exact update was already approved
routes.description: Keep the open packet useful without forcing empty documents or activity-log churn; route each durable change to the clearest canonical concept.

routes.trigger: Work is classified as a durable Manager-native or externally bound project.
routes.approval: dry-run preview then explicit apply approval
routes.description: Create the portable open packet core; bind external implementation work when present, while Manager-native work needs no device binding.

routes.trigger: A recurring collaborator needs durable context.
routes.approval: explicit approval for durable inferred memory
routes.description: Create or update a single canonical evidence-backed note.

routes.trigger: A bounded spike has a clear question and decision criterion but has not earned durable project scope.
routes.approval: dry-run preview then explicit apply approval
routes.description: Create a lightweight Experiment Pack that can be concluded, archived, or promoted without prematurely creating a Project Pack.

routes.trigger: An Experiment Pack becomes a durable multi-session workstream.
routes.approval: dry-run preview then explicit apply approval
routes.description: Preserve the experiment as origin evidence, create a durable Project Pack, and transfer any device-local workspace route.

routes.trigger: A Project or Experiment Pack reaches a meaningful lifecycle boundary.
routes.approval: dry-run preview then explicit apply approval
routes.description: Apply only an allowed Project or Experiment transition; semantic archive and promotion criteria remain agent-judged and reviewable.

routes.trigger: The user explicitly invokes $loop or asks the current task to check, retry, follow up, monitor, or resume on a recurring cadence.
reads: only the sources already approved for the task
writes: optional loop or done task title
routes.approval: the explicit loop request authorizes creation; ask only when cadence or completion is materially ambiguous
routes.description: Translate a plain-language recurring request into one quiet task-attached heartbeat, preserve action boundaries, update rather than duplicate it, and delete it after verified completion.

routes.trigger: The user explicitly invokes UltraGoal or explicitly asks to create, set, start, or activate a persistent goal.
routes.approval: explicit invocation required; implicit activation disabled in agents/openai.yaml
routes.description: Keep persistent goal machinery globally available without triggering it from importance, complexity, or ordinary long-running work.

routes.trigger: WireNet Manager is installed, missing, unhealthy, outdated, or being repaired.
reads: existing Manager when repairing or upgrading
Codex bundled workspace dependencies or executables already on PATH
routes.approval: dry-run first; approval before apply, upgrade, QMD installation, or QMD collection changes
routes.description: Resolve a non-developer local runtime, create or repair the technical baseline separately from the personal first meeting, and never overwrite personalized files during repair.

routes.trigger: A healthy Manager is brand new or its personal first meeting is incomplete.
routes.approval: separate approval for connection, source read, durable write, global instruction, task change, and automation
routes.description: Mirror Jason Liu's first-meeting sequence: greet, show a grounded map, interview one question at a time, perform targeted rereads, propose continuity, and close with a clear handoff.

routes.trigger: The user approves learning their writing voice or migrating an existing repo-local write-like-me profile.
reads: repo-local profile only in current or approved project roots
explicitly approved user-authored sent email or Slack scope when generation or refresh needs it
routes.approval: separate profile migration, source-read, refresh, and generated-skill-write approvals; never overwrite an existing global profile
routes.description: Reuse grounded style evidence when available or infer channel-specific postures without preserving raw private messages; install current valid globally discoverable behavior with explicit implicit-invocation metadata.

routes.trigger: An existing Manager schema is older than the schema supported by the installed plugin.
routes.approval: dry-run preview, explicit approval, clean local Git checkpoint, then post-migration Doctor validation
routes.description: Apply only a supported schema migration while preserving personal concepts and runtime instructions; a plugin update never silently rewrites the workspace.

routes.trigger: A request matches a globally installed WireNet Manager skill.
routes.description: The plugin remains callable outside Manager, enabling cross-workspace classification and reconciliation.

routes.trigger: A task runs in an external workspace and reaches a meaningful handoff, or the global core block requests reconciliation.
writes: approved Project or Experiment Pack diff
routes.approval: preview durable writes; delegate untracked classification
routes.description: Reconcile only meaningful durable progress for an existing tracked Project or Experiment Pack; delegate untracked classification and lifecycle changes to wirenet-manager-project.

routes.trigger: A human, agent, or future sync consumer needs progressive disclosure or graph traversal.
routes.description: Use the deterministic typed-concept projection, reserved indexes, stable metadata, real links, and sparse history without interpreting runtime instructions as knowledge.

routes.trigger: The user asks to inspect Manager visually.
routes.description: Render complete typed concept bodies and real Markdown-link edges in the WireNet Inspector; exclude index.md, log.md, AGENTS.md, implementation, outputs, and local state from the generated payload.

known_ambiguities: The Manager is one OKF bundle plus a transparent AGENTS.md and device-local runtime overlay; only typed concepts and reserved index or log files are knowledge-sync candidates.
Project and Experiment Packs are designed as future synchronization units, but no database or multi-device merge protocol exists in v0.2.
docs/, outputs/, archive/, and notes/ now have explicit WireNet policies; their long-term retention and synchronization behavior needs usage evidence.
Root and project-collection indexes are required by the WireNet profile for stable navigation even though OKF permits them to be absent; packet-local indexes remain optional.
